questions.prompt: The marketing report must be submitted _____ Friday afternoon.
questions.explanationEn: 'By Friday afternoon' means the report must be submitted no later than that time.
questions.explanationKo: 'by Friday afternoon'는 금요일 오후까지, 즉 그 시간보다 늦지 않게 제출해야 한다는 뜻입니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 한국어의 '-까지'는 영어에서 상황에 따라 'until' 또는 'by'가 됩니다. 마감 시간에는 보통 'by'를 씁니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Please submit the report by Friday.
The marketing report is due Friday.
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: マーケティング報告書

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Please submit the report by Friday.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Please submit the report by Friday.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. by です。前置詞は日本語の助詞に直訳せず、後ろの語との組み合わせで判断します。この文では「Friday」が手がかりです。期限なら by、正確な時刻なら at、曜日や日付なら on、範囲や場所なら in のように、英語側の決まった使い方で選びます。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 前置詞は後ろの語とセットで覚えます。「The marketing report must be submitted _____ Friday afternoon.」の空所後の表現が、期限・時刻・場所のどれかを先に分類しましょう。

questions.prompt: Ms. Kim has scheduled _____ interview with the new applicant.
questions.explanationEn: Use 'an' before a vowel sound. 'Interview' starts with a vowel sound.
questions.explanationKo: 'interview'는 모음 소리로 시작하므로 부정관사 'an'을 사용합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 한국어에는 관사가 없기 때문에 영어의 'a/an/the'를 자주 놓칠 수 있습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The applicant arrived early for the interview.
Ms. Kim scheduled an interview.
The new applicant arrived early.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The applicant arrived early for the interview.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. an です。空所の後ろにある名詞句を見ます。この文では「Ms. Kim has scheduled _____ interview with the new applicant.」の空所に an が入り、後ろの名詞の数や音に合います。日本語には冠詞がないため、意味だけでなく名詞が単数か、母音で始まる音か、初出か既出かを確認するのがポイントです。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 空所の直後の名詞を見てください。「Ms. Kim has scheduled _____ interview with the new applicant.」では、名詞が数えられる単数か、母音で始まる音かが決め手になります。

questions.prompt: The supplier _____ the updated invoice yesterday.
questions.explanationEn: 'Yesterday' refers to a finished past time, so the simple past 'sent' is correct.
questions.explanationKo: 'yesterday'는 끝난 과거 시점을 나타내므로 단순과거 'sent'가 맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 한국어는 시제가 문맥으로 표현되는 경우가 많지만, 영어 TOEIC에서는 시간 표현이 동사 시제를 결정하는 핵심 단서입니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The supplier sent the updated invoice.
The supplier sent the invoice.
The updated invoice was sent yesterday.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The supplier sent the updated invoice.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. sent です。時制問題では、文中の時間表現を先に拾います。この文では「yesterday」が動詞形を決める手がかりです。日本語では時制を文脈で補えますが、英語では過去・未来・現在完了などを動詞の形で明示する必要があります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 時制問題では、選択肢を見る前に時間表現を丸で囲えるか確認します。「The supplier _____ the updated invoice yesterday.」の中の過去・未来・継続の合図が得点源です。

questions.prompt: The manager spoke _____ about the new safety policy.
questions.explanationEn: The blank describes how the manager spoke, so an adverb is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 빈칸은 manager가 어떻게 말했는지를 설명하므로 부사 'confidently'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: TOEIC Part 5에서는 품사 문제가 자주 나옵니다. 동사를 꾸미는 자리에는 보통 부사가 필요합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The manager explained the new safety policy.
The manager explained the safety policy.
She spoke confidently about the plan.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The manager explained the new safety policy.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. confidently です。空所が文の中で何を修飾しているかを確認します。「The manager spoke _____ about the new safety policy.」では confidently が周りの語と品詞の役割に合います。動詞を説明する位置なら副詞、名詞を説明する位置なら形容詞、主語や目的語になる位置なら名詞を疑います。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然さより、空所の前後を見ます。「The manager spoke _____ about the new safety policy.」では、空所が動詞・名詞・形容詞のどれを説明しているかで品詞を決めます。

questions.prompt: _____ the weather was severe, the delivery arrived on time.
questions.explanationEn: 'Although' introduces a full clause with a subject and verb.
questions.explanationKo: 'Although' 뒤에는 주어와 동사가 있는 절이 올 수 있습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 'Despite' 뒤에는 명사나 동명사가 오고, 'Although' 뒤에는 절이 옵니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The weather was severe, but the delivery arrived.
The delivery arrived despite severe weather.
The delivery arrived on time.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The weather was severe, but the delivery arrived.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. Although です。接続語は前後の意味関係で選びます。この文では「_____ the weather was severe, the delivery arrived on time.」の二つの内容が、理由・逆接・条件・追加のどれでつながるかを見ます。Although はその関係を自然に示します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 接続語は訳語だけで選ばず、「_____ the weather was severe, the delivery arrived on time.」の前半と後半が逆接・理由・条件・追加のどれかを先に決めましょう。

questions.prompt: The customer service team responded _____ to all complaints.
questions.explanationEn: The blank describes how the team responded, so the adverb 'promptly' is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 빈칸은 팀이 어떻게 응답했는지를 설명하므로 부사 'promptly'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 동사를 꾸미는 자리에는 형용사가 아니라 부사가 오는 경우가 많습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The team responded promptly to the complaint.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The team responded promptly to the complaint.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. promptly です。空所が文の中で何を修飾しているかを確認します。「The customer service team responded _____ to all complaints.」では promptly が周りの語と品詞の役割に合います。動詞を説明する位置なら副詞、名詞を説明する位置なら形容詞、主語や目的語になる位置なら名詞を疑います。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然さより、空所の前後を見ます。「The customer service team responded _____ to all complaints.」では、空所が動詞・名詞・形容詞のどれを説明しているかで品詞を決めます。

questions.prompt: The training session will begin _____ 9:30 A.M.
questions.explanationEn: Use 'at' with exact times such as 9:30 A.M.
questions.explanationKo: 9:30 A.M.처럼 정확한 시간 앞에는 전치사 'at'을 사용합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 영어에서는 시간 표현의 종류에 따라 at, on, in을 구분해야 합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The training session begins at 9:30 A.M.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The training session begins at 9:30 A.M.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. at です。前置詞は日本語の助詞に直訳せず、後ろの語との組み合わせで判断します。この文では「9:30」が手がかりです。期限なら by、正確な時刻なら at、曜日や日付なら on、範囲や場所なら in のように、英語側の決まった使い方で選びます。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 前置詞は後ろの語とセットで覚えます。「The training session will begin _____ 9:30 A.M.」の空所後の表現が、期限・時刻・場所のどれかを先に分類しましょう。

questions.prompt: The company opened _____ new branch near Busan Station.
questions.explanationEn: Use 'a' before a singular countable noun when introducing it for the first time.
questions.explanationKo: 처음 언급하는 단수 가산명사 앞에는 부정관사 'a'를 사용합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 한국어에는 관사가 없기 때문에 branch 같은 단수 가산명사 앞 관사를 놓치기 쉽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The company opened a new branch.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The company opened a new branch.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. a です。空所の後ろにある名詞句を見ます。この文では「The company opened _____ new branch near Busan Station.」の空所に a が入り、後ろの名詞の数や音に合います。日本語には冠詞がないため、意味だけでなく名詞が単数か、母音で始まる音か、初出か既出かを確認するのがポイントです。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 空所の直後の名詞を見てください。「The company opened _____ new branch near Busan Station.」では、名詞が数えられる単数か、母音で始まる音かが決め手になります。

questions.prompt: The accounting department _____ the budget before the meeting starts.
questions.explanationEn: The action is expected to happen before a future meeting, so 'will review' fits.
questions.explanationKo: 회의가 시작되기 전에 일어날 미래의 일을 말하므로 'will review'가 자연스럽습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: before가 있어도 문맥이 미래이면 미래 시제를 선택해야 합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The department will review the budget.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The department will review the budget.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. will review です。時制問題では、文中の時間表現を先に拾います。この文では「before」が動詞形を決める手がかりです。日本語では時制を文脈で補えますが、英語では過去・未来・現在完了などを動詞の形で明示する必要があります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 時制問題では、選択肢を見る前に時間表現を丸で囲えるか確認します。「The accounting department _____ the budget before the meeting starts.」の中の過去・未来・継続の合図が得点源です。

questions.prompt: The shipment was delayed _____ the warehouse had already prepared the order.
questions.explanationEn: 'Even though' shows contrast between the delay and the prepared order.
questions.explanationKo: 배송 지연과 이미 준비된 주문 사이의 대조를 나타내므로 'even though'가 맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 대조 관계인지 원인 관계인지 먼저 파악하면 접속사 문제를 풀기 쉽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The shipment was delayed.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The shipment was delayed.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. even though です。接続語は前後の意味関係で選びます。この文では「The shipment was delayed _____ the warehouse had already prepared the order.」の二つの内容が、理由・逆接・条件・追加のどれでつながるかを見ます。even though はその関係を自然に示します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 接続語は訳語だけで選ばず、「The shipment was delayed _____ the warehouse had already prepared the order.」の前半と後半が逆接・理由・条件・追加のどれかを先に決めましょう。

questions.prompt: Employees must _____ their identification badges while inside the building.
questions.explanationEn: You 'wear' a badge. The other choices do not collocate with identification badges.
questions.explanationKo: 배지는 'wear'한다고 표현합니다. 다른 선택지는 identification badges와 어울리지 않습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: TOEIC에서는 단어 뜻뿐 아니라 함께 쓰이는 표현(collocation)이 중요합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Employees must wear identification badges.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Employees must wear identification badges.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. wear です。語彙問題では意味だけでなく、周りの名詞やビジネス場面との相性を見ます。「Employees must _____ their identification badges while inside the building.」では wear が文脈と自然に合います。ほかの選択肢は似た形でも、目的語や場面との組み合わせが弱くなります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「Employees must _____ their identification badges while inside the building.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: This year's conference room is _____ than the one we used last year.
questions.explanationEn: 'Than' signals a comparative form, so 'more spacious' is correct.
questions.explanationKo: 'than'이 있으므로 비교급 'more spacious'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: than은 비교급을 찾으라는 강한 단서입니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The conference room is more spacious.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The conference room is more spacious.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. more spacious です。比較問題では than や as ... as のような比較の合図を探します。「This year's conference room is _____ than the one we used last year.」では比較対象が示されているため、原級や最上級ではなく比較級が必要です。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「This year's conference room is _____ than the one we used last year.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: Each employee should submit _____ timesheet by Friday.
questions.explanationEn: 'Each employee' is singular, so a singular possessive phrase is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 'Each employee'는 단수 취급하므로 단수 소유 표현이 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: each, every 뒤의 명사는 단수로 보고 동사와 대명사를 맞춰야 합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Each employee should submit a timesheet.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Each employee should submit a timesheet.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. his or her です。代名詞は、前に出た名詞の単数・複数や人称に合わせます。「Each employee should submit _____ timesheet by Friday.」では、代名詞が何を受けるのかを先に決める必要があります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語では省略される主語や数も、英語では形に出ます。「Each employee should submit _____ timesheet by Friday.」では中心になる名詞を探してから選択肢を見ましょう。

questions.prompt: Visitors _____ sign in at the reception desk before entering.
questions.explanationEn: 'Must' expresses a requirement, which fits the visitor rule.
questions.explanationKo: 방문객 규칙처럼 의무를 나타낼 때는 'must'가 적절합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 규칙, 안내문, 사내 공지에서는 must, should, are required to가 자주 나옵니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Visitors must sign in at the reception desk.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Visitors must sign in at the reception desk.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. must です。動詞の後ろに続く形は、動詞ごとのパターンで決まります。「Visitors _____ sign in at the reception desk before entering.」では must の形が文型に合います。to 不定詞、原形、現在分詞、過去分詞のどれが必要かを、意味だけでなく構造から判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「Visitors _____ sign in at the reception desk before entering.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The list of available rooms _____ posted near the elevator.
questions.explanationEn: The subject is 'list', which is singular, so 'is' is correct.
questions.explanationKo: 주어는 'rooms'가 아니라 단수명사 'list'이므로 'is'가 맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 전치사구 of available rooms에 속지 말고 진짜 주어를 찾으세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The list of available rooms is posted.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The list of available rooms is posted.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. is です。主語と動詞の一致では、動詞に対応する本当の主語を探します。「The list of available rooms _____ posted near the elevator.」では、途中の of 句や説明語に引っ張られず、中心名詞が単数か複数かを確認します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語では省略される主語や数も、英語では形に出ます。「The list of available rooms _____ posted near the elevator.」では中心になる名詞を探してから選択肢を見ましょう。

questions.prompt: The director asked the team _____ the proposal by Monday.
questions.explanationEn: The pattern is 'ask someone to do something'.
questions.explanationKo: 'ask someone to do something' 구조이므로 'to revise'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 동사 패턴을 외우면 Part 5에서 빠르게 답을 고를 수 있습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The team needs to revise the proposal.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The team needs to revise the proposal.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. to revise です。動詞の後ろに続く形は、動詞ごとのパターンで決まります。「The director asked the team _____ the proposal by Monday.」では to revise の形が文型に合います。to 不定詞、原形、現在分詞、過去分詞のどれが必要かを、意味だけでなく構造から判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The director asked the team _____ the proposal by Monday.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The _____ documents should be placed in the archive room.
questions.explanationEn: Documents that have received approval are 'approved documents'.
questions.explanationKo: 승인된 문서를 의미하므로 과거분사 'approved'가 맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 명사가 동작을 받는 관계이면 과거분사를 쓰는 경우가 많습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The documents are in the archive room.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The documents are in the archive room.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. approved です。動詞の後ろに続く形は、動詞ごとのパターンで決まります。「The _____ documents should be placed in the archive room.」では approved の形が文型に合います。to 不定詞、原形、現在分詞、過去分詞のどれが必要かを、意味だけでなく構造から判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The _____ documents should be placed in the archive room.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The new printer operates very _____.
questions.explanationEn: The blank modifies the verb 'operates', so an adverb is required.
questions.explanationKo: 빈칸은 동사 'operates'를 꾸미므로 부사 'quietly'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 동사 뒤에서 방식이나 정도를 설명하면 부사를 먼저 의심하세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The printer operates quietly.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The printer operates quietly.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. quietly です。空所が文の中で何を修飾しているかを確認します。「The new printer operates very _____.」では quietly が周りの語と品詞の役割に合います。動詞を説明する位置なら副詞、名詞を説明する位置なら形容詞、主語や目的語になる位置なら名詞を疑います。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然さより、空所の前後を見ます。「The new printer operates very _____.」では、空所が動詞・名詞・形容詞のどれを説明しているかで品詞を決めます。

questions.prompt: The hotel offers a _____ shuttle service to the airport.
questions.explanationEn: 'Complimentary' means free, which fits a hotel shuttle service.
questions.explanationKo: 'complimentary'는 무료라는 뜻으로 호텔 셔틀 서비스와 잘 어울립니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 비슷하게 생긴 단어를 뜻으로 구분하는 문제는 TOEIC에서 자주 나옵니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The hotel offers a complimentary shuttle.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The hotel offers a complimentary shuttle.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. complimentary です。語彙問題では意味だけでなく、周りの名詞やビジネス場面との相性を見ます。「The hotel offers a _____ shuttle service to the airport.」では complimentary が文脈と自然に合います。ほかの選択肢は似た形でも、目的語や場面との組み合わせが弱くなります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The hotel offers a _____ shuttle service to the airport.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: If the machine _____ again, please contact maintenance immediately.
questions.explanationEn: In a real future condition, use the present simple after 'if'.
questions.explanationKo: 실현 가능한 미래 조건에서는 if절에 현재시제를 사용하므로 'fails'가 맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: if절 안에 미래 의미가 있어도 보통 will을 쓰지 않고 현재시제를 씁니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Please contact maintenance immediately.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Please contact maintenance immediately.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. fails です。「If the machine _____ again, please contact maintenance immediately.」で問われている文法機能を確認し、英語の語順・品詞・決まった表現に合う選択肢を選びます。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「If the machine _____ again, please contact maintenance immediately.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The revised schedule will be posted _____ the employee bulletin board.
questions.explanationEn: Use 'on' when information is displayed on a board, wall, or screen.
questions.explanationKo: 게시판이나 벽, 화면 위에 정보가 표시될 때는 전치사 'on'을 사용합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 한국어의 '-에'는 여러 의미로 쓰이지만, 영어에서는 표면 위에 붙어 있거나 표시된 정보에는 보통 'on'을 씁니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The revised schedule will be posted today.
Please check the bulletin board for updates.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The revised schedule will be posted today.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. on です。前置詞は日本語の助詞に直訳せず、後ろの語との組み合わせで判断します。この文では「The revised schedule will be posted _____ the employee bulletin board.」が手がかりです。期限なら by、正確な時刻なら at、曜日や日付なら on、範囲や場所なら in のように、英語側の決まった使い方で選びます。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 前置詞は後ろの語とセットで覚えます。「The revised schedule will be posted _____ the employee bulletin board.」の空所後の表現が、期限・時刻・場所のどれかを先に分類しましょう。

questions.prompt: The receptionist greeted each visitor _____.
questions.explanationEn: The blank describes how the receptionist greeted visitors, so an adverb is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 빈칸은 접수 담당자가 방문객을 어떻게 맞이했는지를 설명하므로 부사 'courteously'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 동사를 꾸미는 자리에는 형용사가 아니라 부사가 오는 경우가 많습니다. Part 5에서는 품사 위치를 먼저 확인하세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The receptionist greeted each visitor.
The staff spoke courteously to customers.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The receptionist greeted each visitor.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. courteously です。空所が文の中で何を修飾しているかを確認します。「The receptionist greeted each visitor _____.」では courteously が周りの語と品詞の役割に合います。動詞を説明する位置なら副詞、名詞を説明する位置なら形容詞、主語や目的語になる位置なら名詞を疑います。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然さより、空所の前後を見ます。「The receptionist greeted each visitor _____.」では、空所が動詞・名詞・形容詞のどれを説明しているかで品詞を決めます。

questions.prompt: By the time the conference begins, all name tags _____.
questions.explanationEn: 'By the time' with a future reference often requires the future perfect for an action completed before that point.
questions.explanationKo: 미래의 특정 시점 전에 완료될 일을 나타내므로 미래완료 수동형 'will have been prepared'가 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 'by the time'은 완료 시점을 알려 주는 단서입니다. 이름표는 준비되는 대상이므로 수동형도 함께 필요합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The conference begins at nine.
All name tags will be prepared in advance.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The conference begins at nine.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. will have been prepared です。時制問題では、文中の時間表現を先に拾います。この文では「By the time the conference begins, all name tags _____.」が動詞形を決める手がかりです。日本語では時制を文脈で補えますが、英語では過去・未来・現在完了などを動詞の形で明示する必要があります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 時制問題では、選択肢を見る前に時間表現を丸で囲えるか確認します。「By the time the conference begins, all name tags _____.」の中の過去・未来・継続の合図が得点源です。

questions.prompt: Please contact the supervisor _____ you need additional safety equipment.
questions.explanationEn: 'If' introduces a condition: contact the supervisor in the situation that equipment is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 추가 안전 장비가 필요한 경우라는 조건을 나타내므로 'if'가 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 조건을 나타내는 '-면'은 영어에서 보통 'if'로 연결합니다. 뒤에 주어와 동사가 있는 절이 올 수 있습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Please contact the supervisor.
Workers must wear safety equipment.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Please contact the supervisor.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. if です。接続語は前後の意味関係で選びます。この文では「Please contact the supervisor _____ you need additional safety equipment.」の二つの内容が、理由・逆接・条件・追加のどれでつながるかを見ます。if はその関係を自然に示します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 接続語は訳語だけで選ばず、「Please contact the supervisor _____ you need additional safety equipment.」の前半と後半が逆接・理由・条件・追加のどれかを先に決めましょう。

questions.prompt: Our department is looking for _____ experienced technician.
questions.explanationEn: Use 'an' before a vowel sound. 'Experienced' begins with a vowel sound.
questions.explanationKo: 'experienced'는 모음 소리로 시작하므로 부정관사 'an'을 사용합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 관사는 철자가 아니라 발음으로 판단해야 합니다. 모음 소리 앞에는 'an'이 옵니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: They hired an experienced technician.
The technician repaired the machine.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: They hired an experienced technician.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. an です。空所の後ろにある名詞句を見ます。この文では「Our department is looking for _____ experienced technician.」の空所に an が入り、後ろの名詞の数や音に合います。日本語には冠詞がないため、意味だけでなく名詞が単数か、母音で始まる音か、初出か既出かを確認するのがポイントです。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 空所の直後の名詞を見てください。「Our department is looking for _____ experienced technician.」では、名詞が数えられる単数か、母音で始まる音かが決め手になります。

questions.prompt: Each of the applicants _____ required to submit two references.
questions.explanationEn: 'Each' is singular, so the singular verb 'is' is required.
questions.explanationKo: 'Each'는 단수 취급하므로 단수 동사 'is'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 'of the applicants'에 복수 명사가 있어도 문장의 핵심 주어는 'Each'입니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Each applicant must submit references.
Please submit two references.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Each applicant must submit references.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. is です。主語と動詞の一致では、動詞に対応する本当の主語を探します。「Each of the applicants _____ required to submit two references.」では、途中の of 句や説明語に引っ張られず、中心名詞が単数か複数かを確認します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語では省略される主語や数も、英語では形に出ます。「Each of the applicants _____ required to submit two references.」では中心になる名詞を探してから選択肢を見ましょう。

questions.prompt: The factory will _____ production next week after the equipment inspection.
questions.explanationEn: 'Resume production' means to start production again after a pause.
questions.explanationKo: 'resume production'은 중단되었던 생산을 다시 시작한다는 뜻입니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 'resume'은 명사로는 이력서라는 뜻도 있지만, 동사로는 '다시 시작하다'라는 뜻이 TOEIC에서 자주 나옵니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The factory will resume production next week.
The inspection will finish on Friday.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The factory will resume production next week.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. resume です。語彙問題では意味だけでなく、周りの名詞やビジネス場面との相性を見ます。「The factory will _____ production next week after the equipment inspection.」では resume が文脈と自然に合います。ほかの選択肢は似た形でも、目的語や場面との組み合わせが弱くなります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The factory will _____ production next week after the equipment inspection.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The online order was processed almost _____ after payment was confirmed.
questions.explanationEn: The adverb 'immediately' describes how quickly the order was processed.
questions.explanationKo: 주문이 처리된 방식을 설명하므로 부사 'immediately'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 'almost' 뒤에도 동작을 설명하는 부사가 올 수 있습니다. 선택지의 품사를 비교하세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The order was processed immediately.
Payment was confirmed this morning.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The order was processed immediately.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. immediately です。空所が文の中で何を修飾しているかを確認します。「The online order was processed almost _____ after payment was confirmed.」では immediately が周りの語と品詞の役割に合います。動詞を説明する位置なら副詞、名詞を説明する位置なら形容詞、主語や目的語になる位置なら名詞を疑います。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然さより、空所の前後を見ます。「The online order was processed almost _____ after payment was confirmed.」では、空所が動詞・名詞・形容詞のどれを説明しているかで品詞を決めます。

questions.prompt: The marketing team shared _____ final proposal with the client.
questions.explanationEn: A possessive adjective is needed before the noun phrase 'final proposal.'
questions.explanationKo: 명사구 'final proposal' 앞에는 소유를 나타내는 형용사 'their'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 빈칸 뒤에 명사가 바로 오면 소유격 형용사인지 확인하세요. 'they'나 'them'은 명사를 직접 꾸미지 못합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The team shared their final proposal.
The client reviewed the proposal.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The team shared their final proposal.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. their です。代名詞は、前に出た名詞の単数・複数や人称に合わせます。「The marketing team shared _____ final proposal with the client.」では、代名詞が何を受けるのかを先に決める必要があります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語では省略される主語や数も、英語では形に出ます。「The marketing team shared _____ final proposal with the client.」では中心になる名詞を探してから選択肢を見ましょう。

questions.prompt: This printer is _____ than the model we purchased last year.
questions.explanationEn: 'Than' signals a comparison between two items, so the comparative form is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 'than'이 두 대상을 비교하고 있으므로 비교급 'more efficient'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 긴 형용사는 보통 '-er' 대신 'more + 형용사'로 비교급을 만듭니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: This printer is more efficient.
The new model uses less electricity.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: This printer is more efficient.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. more efficient です。比較問題では than や as ... as のような比較の合図を探します。「This printer is _____ than the model we purchased last year.」では比較対象が示されているため、原級や最上級ではなく比較級が必要です。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「This printer is _____ than the model we purchased last year.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The training video explains how _____ the new inventory system.
questions.explanationEn: After 'how,' use the infinitive form 'to use' to explain a method.
questions.explanationKo: 방법을 설명하는 'how' 뒤에는 부정사 'to use'를 사용할 수 있습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 'how to + 동사원형'은 '~하는 방법'이라는 TOEIC 빈출 구조입니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The video explains how to use the system.
Please watch the training video.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The video explains how to use the system.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. to use です。動詞の後ろに続く形は、動詞ごとのパターンで決まります。「The training video explains how _____ the new inventory system.」では to use の形が文型に合います。to 不定詞、原形、現在分詞、過去分詞のどれが必要かを、意味だけでなく構造から判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The training video explains how _____ the new inventory system.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: Visitors _____ sign in at the front desk before entering the laboratory.
questions.explanationEn: 'Must' expresses a requirement, which fits the sign-in rule.
questions.explanationKo: 방문객이 반드시 서명해야 한다는 규칙을 나타내므로 'must'가 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 규칙이나 의무를 나타내는 문장에서는 'must' 또는 'have to'가 자주 정답이 됩니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Visitors must sign in at the front desk.
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: 署名して入る、チェックインする

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Visitors cannot enter the laboratory alone.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Visitors must sign in at the front desk.
vocabularyItem.meaningJa: 署名して入る、チェックインする

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. must です。動詞の後ろに続く形は、動詞ごとのパターンで決まります。「Visitors _____ sign in at the front desk before entering the laboratory.」では must の形が文型に合います。to 不定詞、原形、現在分詞、過去分詞のどれが必要かを、意味だけでなく構造から判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「Visitors _____ sign in at the front desk before entering the laboratory.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The documents _____ in the blue folder are ready for review.
questions.explanationEn: 'Placed in the blue folder' is a reduced passive clause describing the documents.
questions.explanationKo: 'placed in the blue folder'는 문서를 설명하는 축약된 수동 분사구입니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 문서가 폴더에 '놓여진' 상태이므로 현재분사보다 과거분사가 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The documents are ready for review.
The report is ready for review.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The documents are ready for review.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. placed です。動詞の後ろに続く形は、動詞ごとのパターンで決まります。「The documents _____ in the blue folder are ready for review.」では placed の形が文型に合います。to 不定詞、原形、現在分詞、過去分詞のどれが必要かを、意味だけでなく構造から判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The documents _____ in the blue folder are ready for review.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: If the shipment arrives before noon, the staff _____ the items today.
questions.explanationEn: In a real future condition, use present tense in the if-clause and 'will' in the main clause.
questions.explanationKo: 실현 가능한 미래 조건문에서는 if절에 현재형을 쓰고, 주절에는 'will'을 사용합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 영어 조건문에서는 if절에 미래 의미가 있어도 보통 'will'을 넣지 않습니다. 주절에서 미래를 표시합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The shipment arrives before noon.
The staff will distribute the items.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The shipment arrives before noon.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. will distribute です。「If the shipment arrives before noon, the staff _____ the items today.」で問われている文法機能を確認し、英語の語順・品詞・決まった表現に合う選択肢を選びます。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「If the shipment arrives before noon, the staff _____ the items today.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The hotel offers a _____ shuttle service to the airport every hour.
questions.explanationEn: 'Complimentary' means free of charge, which fits a hotel shuttle service.
questions.explanationKo: 'complimentary'는 무료 제공이라는 뜻으로 호텔 셔틀 서비스 문맥에 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 'complimentary'는 '칭찬하는'이라는 뜻도 있지만, TOEIC 비즈니스 문맥에서는 '무료의'라는 뜻으로 자주 쓰입니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The hotel offers complimentary breakfast.
A shuttle service runs every hour.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The hotel offers complimentary breakfast.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. complimentary です。語彙問題では意味だけでなく、周りの名詞やビジネス場面との相性を見ます。「The hotel offers a _____ shuttle service to the airport every hour.」では complimentary が文脈と自然に合います。ほかの選択肢は似た形でも、目的語や場面との組み合わせが弱くなります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The hotel offers a _____ shuttle service to the airport every hour.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The finance director is responsible _____ approving travel expenses.
questions.explanationEn: The expression is 'be responsible for' something or doing something.
questions.explanationKo: '~을 책임지다'는 영어로 'be responsible for'라고 표현합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 형용사와 함께 쓰이는 전치사는 세트로 외우는 것이 좋습니다. 'responsible for'는 TOEIC에서 자주 나옵니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: She is responsible for approving expenses.
Travel expenses must be approved.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: She is responsible for approving expenses.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. for です。前置詞は日本語の助詞に直訳せず、後ろの語との組み合わせで判断します。この文では「The finance director is responsible _____ approving travel expenses.」が手がかりです。期限なら by、正確な時刻なら at、曜日や日付なら on、範囲や場所なら in のように、英語側の決まった使い方で選びます。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 前置詞は後ろの語とセットで覚えます。「The finance director is responsible _____ approving travel expenses.」の空所後の表現が、期限・時刻・場所のどれかを先に分類しましょう。

questions.prompt: The consultant's recommendations were both practical and _____.
questions.explanationEn: The blank is parallel with the adjective 'practical,' so another adjective is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 빈칸은 형용사 'practical'과 병렬 구조이므로 또 다른 형용사 'affordable'이 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 'both A and B' 구조에서는 A와 B의 품사가 같아야 합니다. 병렬 구조를 확인하세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The consultant made several recommendations.
The plan is practical and affordable.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The consultant made several recommendations.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. affordable です。空所が文の中で何を修飾しているかを確認します。「The consultant's recommendations were both practical and _____.」では affordable が周りの語と品詞の役割に合います。動詞を説明する位置なら副詞、名詞を説明する位置なら形容詞、主語や目的語になる位置なら名詞を疑います。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然さより、空所の前後を見ます。「The consultant's recommendations were both practical and _____.」では、空所が動詞・名詞・形容詞のどれを説明しているかで品詞を決めます。

questions.prompt: The store will remain open late _____ the renovation work is completed.
questions.explanationEn: 'Even if' means the store will remain open late regardless of whether the work is completed.
questions.explanationKo: 공사가 완료되더라도 늦게까지 영업한다는 양보의 의미이므로 'even if'가 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 'even if'는 실제 여부와 관계없이 조건을 인정하는 표현입니다. 단순한 시간 표현인 'as soon as'와 구분하세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The store will remain open late.
The renovation work is almost complete.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The store will remain open late.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. even if です。接続語は前後の意味関係で選びます。この文では「The store will remain open late _____ the renovation work is completed.」の二つの内容が、理由・逆接・条件・追加のどれでつながるかを見ます。even if はその関係を自然に示します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 接続語は訳語だけで選ばず、「The store will remain open late _____ the renovation work is completed.」の前半と後半が逆接・理由・条件・追加のどれかを先に決めましょう。

questions.prompt: The customer survey results were _____ positive.
questions.explanationEn: 'Highly' modifies the adjective 'positive.'
questions.explanationKo: 'positive'라는 형용사를 꾸미는 부사 'highly'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 형용사를 꾸밀 때도 부사가 필요합니다. 'high positive'가 아니라 'highly positive'입니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The survey results were highly positive.
The feedback was positive.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The survey results were highly positive.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. highly です。空所が文の中で何を修飾しているかを確認します。「The customer survey results were _____ positive.」では highly が周りの語と品詞の役割に合います。動詞を説明する位置なら副詞、名詞を説明する位置なら形容詞、主語や目的語になる位置なら名詞を疑います。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然さより、空所の前後を見ます。「The customer survey results were _____ positive.」では、空所が動詞・名詞・形容詞のどれを説明しているかで品詞を決めます。

questions.prompt: To reduce errors, the warehouse introduced a more _____ labeling process.
questions.explanationEn: 'Accurate' means correct or exact, which fits a process designed to reduce errors.
questions.explanationKo: 'accurate'는 정확한이라는 뜻으로 오류를 줄이기 위한 라벨링 절차 문맥에 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 비슷하게 시작하는 형용사 선택지는 뜻을 정확히 구분해야 합니다. 오류를 줄이는 목적에는 'accurate'가 가장 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The new process reduces errors.
The labeling process is more accurate now.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The new process reduces errors.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. accurate です。語彙問題では意味だけでなく、周りの名詞やビジネス場面との相性を見ます。「To reduce errors, the warehouse introduced a more _____ labeling process.」では accurate が文脈と自然に合います。ほかの選択肢は似た形でも、目的語や場面との組み合わせが弱くなります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「To reduce errors, the warehouse introduced a more _____ labeling process.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The payroll department handles salary information _____.
questions.explanationEn: The blank describes how the department handles information, so the adverb 'confidentially' is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 빈칸은 부서가 급여 정보를 어떻게 다루는지를 설명하므로 부사 'confidentially'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 동사를 꾸미는 자리에는 부사가 옵니다. TOEIC Part 5에서는 품사 위치를 먼저 확인하세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The payroll department handles salary information.
Employee records must be handled confidentially.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The payroll department handles salary information.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. confidentially です。空所が文の中で何を修飾しているかを確認します。「The payroll department handles salary information _____.」では confidentially が周りの語と品詞の役割に合います。動詞を説明する位置なら副詞、名詞を説明する位置なら形容詞、主語や目的語になる位置なら名詞を疑います。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然さより、空所の前後を見ます。「The payroll department handles salary information _____.」では、空所が動詞・名詞・形容詞のどれを説明しているかで品詞を決めます。

questions.prompt: The new employee orientation will take place _____ the auditorium.
questions.explanationEn: Use 'in' for an event located inside a room or building such as an auditorium.
questions.explanationKo: 강당처럼 실내 공간 안에서 행사가 열릴 때는 전치사 'in'을 사용합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 장소 전치사는 공간의 성격을 봐야 합니다. 실내 공간 안이면 보통 'in'이 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Orientation will take place in the auditorium.
The auditorium is on the first floor.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Orientation will take place in the auditorium.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. in です。前置詞は日本語の助詞に直訳せず、後ろの語との組み合わせで判断します。この文では「The new employee orientation will take place _____ the auditorium.」が手がかりです。期限なら by、正確な時刻なら at、曜日や日付なら on、範囲や場所なら in のように、英語側の決まった使い方で選びます。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 前置詞は後ろの語とセットで覚えます。「The new employee orientation will take place _____ the auditorium.」の空所後の表現が、期限・時刻・場所のどれかを先に分類しましょう。

questions.prompt: The legal team _____ the revised contract by the end of the week.
questions.explanationEn: 'By the end of the week' points to completion before a future deadline, so the future perfect is appropriate.
questions.explanationKo: 'by the end of the week'는 미래의 마감 시점 전 완료를 나타내므로 미래완료형이 적절합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 'by + 미래 시점'은 완료 시제를 고르는 강한 단서가 될 수 있습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The legal team reviewed the contract.
The revised contract is ready.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The legal team reviewed the contract.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. will have reviewed です。時制問題では、文中の時間表現を先に拾います。この文では「The legal team _____ the revised contract by the end of the week.」が動詞形を決める手がかりです。日本語では時制を文脈で補えますが、英語では過去・未来・現在完了などを動詞の形で明示する必要があります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 時制問題では、選択肢を見る前に時間表現を丸で囲えるか確認します。「The legal team _____ the revised contract by the end of the week.」の中の過去・未来・継続の合図が得点源です。

questions.prompt: Mr. Patel gave _____ detailed explanation of the new billing system.
questions.explanationEn: 'Detailed explanation' is a singular countable noun phrase introduced for the first time, so 'a' is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 'detailed explanation'은 처음 언급되는 단수 가산 명사구이므로 'a'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 형용사가 붙어도 핵심 명사가 단수 가산명사이면 관사가 필요합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: He gave a detailed explanation.
The billing system was updated.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: He gave a detailed explanation.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. a です。空所の後ろにある名詞句を見ます。この文では「Mr. Patel gave _____ detailed explanation of the new billing system.」の空所に a が入り、後ろの名詞の数や音に合います。日本語には冠詞がないため、意味だけでなく名詞が単数か、母音で始まる音か、初出か既出かを確認するのがポイントです。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 空所の直後の名詞を見てください。「Mr. Patel gave _____ detailed explanation of the new billing system.」では、名詞が数えられる単数か、母音で始まる音かが決め手になります。

questions.prompt: The museum will extend its hours _____ visitor demand continues to increase.
questions.explanationEn: 'If' introduces the condition under which the museum will extend its hours.
questions.explanationKo: 방문객 수요가 계속 증가한다는 조건을 나타내므로 'if'가 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 조건을 나타내는 절에는 'if'가 자주 쓰입니다. 결과는 주절에 나옵니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The museum will extend its hours.
Visitor demand has increased.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The museum will extend its hours.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. if です。接続語は前後の意味関係で選びます。この文では「The museum will extend its hours _____ visitor demand continues to increase.」の二つの内容が、理由・逆接・条件・追加のどれでつながるかを見ます。if はその関係を自然に示します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 接続語は訳語だけで選ばず、「The museum will extend its hours _____ visitor demand continues to increase.」の前半と後半が逆接・理由・条件・追加のどれかを先に決めましょう。

questions.prompt: All workshop participants will receive a certificate of _____.
questions.explanationEn: A 'certificate of completion' is given after finishing a course or workshop.
questions.explanationKo: 'certificate of completion'은 과정이나 워크숍을 마친 뒤 받는 수료증이라는 뜻입니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: TOEIC에서는 명사와 명사의 결합 표현이 자주 나옵니다. certificate of completion을 통째로 익히세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Participants receive a certificate of completion.
Workshop participants signed in at nine.
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: ワークショップ参加者

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Participants receive a certificate of completion.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. completion です。語彙問題では意味だけでなく、周りの名詞やビジネス場面との相性を見ます。「All workshop participants will receive a certificate of _____.」では completion が文脈と自然に合います。ほかの選択肢は似た形でも、目的語や場面との組み合わせが弱くなります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「All workshop participants will receive a certificate of _____.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: Neither the manager nor the assistants _____ available for the call.
questions.explanationEn: With 'neither...nor,' the verb often agrees with the nearer subject. 'Assistants' is plural, so 'are' is correct.
questions.explanationKo: 'neither...nor'에서는 가까운 주어에 동사를 맞추는 경우가 많습니다. 가까운 주어 'assistants'가 복수이므로 'are'가 맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 상관접속사 문제에서는 동사와 가장 가까운 주어를 확인하세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The assistants are available for the call.
Neither the manager nor the assistants are available.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The assistants are available for the call.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. are です。主語と動詞の一致では、動詞に対応する本当の主語を探します。「Neither the manager nor the assistants _____ available for the call.」では、途中の of 句や説明語に引っ張られず、中心名詞が単数か複数かを確認します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語では省略される主語や数も、英語では形に出ます。「Neither the manager nor the assistants _____ available for the call.」では中心になる名詞を探してから選択肢を見ましょう。

questions.prompt: The safety manual is available online, and employees should read _____ before Friday.
questions.explanationEn: 'It' refers to the singular noun phrase 'the safety manual.'
questions.explanationKo: 'it'은 단수 명사구 'the safety manual'을 가리킵니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 대명사 문제에서는 앞에 나온 명사의 단수/복수 여부를 확인하세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Employees should read the safety manual.
The manual is available online.
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: オンラインで利用可能な

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Employees should read the safety manual.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. it です。代名詞は、前に出た名詞の単数・複数や人称に合わせます。「The safety manual is available online, and employees should read _____ before Friday.」では、代名詞が何を受けるのかを先に決める必要があります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語では省略される主語や数も、英語では形に出ます。「The safety manual is available online, and employees should read _____ before Friday.」では中心になる名詞を探してから選択肢を見ましょう。

questions.prompt: Employees _____ use personal devices to access confidential client files.
questions.explanationEn: 'Must not' expresses prohibition, which fits a rule about confidential files.
questions.explanationKo: 기밀 고객 파일 접근에 대한 금지 규칙이므로 'must not'이 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 규칙에서 금지를 나타낼 때는 'must not' 또는 'may not'이 자주 쓰입니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Do not use personal devices for client files.
Confidential files require secure access.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Do not use personal devices for client files.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. must not です。動詞の後ろに続く形は、動詞ごとのパターンで決まります。「Employees _____ use personal devices to access confidential client files.」では must not の形が文型に合います。to 不定詞、原形、現在分詞、過去分詞のどれが必要かを、意味だけでなく構造から判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「Employees _____ use personal devices to access confidential client files.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The updated checkout process is significantly _____ than the previous version.
questions.explanationEn: 'Than' signals a comparison, so the comparative form 'faster' is required.
questions.explanationKo: 'than'이 비교를 나타내므로 비교급 'faster'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: than이 보이면 비교급 형태를 먼저 의심하세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The checkout process is faster now.
The previous version was slower.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The checkout process is faster now.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. faster です。比較問題では than や as ... as のような比較の合図を探します。「The updated checkout process is significantly _____ than the previous version.」では比較対象が示されているため、原級や最上級ではなく比較級が必要です。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The updated checkout process is significantly _____ than the previous version.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The proposal _____ by the planning committee includes several cost-saving measures.
questions.explanationEn: 'Approved by the planning committee' is a reduced passive clause describing the proposal.
questions.explanationKo: 'approved by the planning committee'는 제안서를 설명하는 축약된 수동 분사구입니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 제안서가 승인하는 것이 아니라 승인된 대상이므로 과거분사가 필요합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The planning committee approved the proposal.
The proposal includes cost-saving measures.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The planning committee approved the proposal.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. approved です。動詞の後ろに続く形は、動詞ごとのパターンで決まります。「The proposal _____ by the planning committee includes several cost-saving measures.」では approved の形が文型に合います。to 不定詞、原形、現在分詞、過去分詞のどれが必要かを、意味だけでなく構造から判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The proposal _____ by the planning committee includes several cost-saving measures.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The supervisor reminded the interns _____ their ID badges at all times.
questions.explanationEn: The structure is 'remind someone to do something.'
questions.explanationKo: 'remind someone to do something' 구조이므로 'to wear'가 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 동사별로 뒤에 오는 형태가 정해져 있습니다. remind는 목적어 뒤에 to부정사를 자주 씁니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: She reminded interns to wear badges.
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: 人に〜するよう念を押す

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Wear your badge at all times.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: She reminded interns to wear badges.
vocabularyItem.meaningJa: 人に〜するよう念を押す

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. to wear です。動詞の後ろに続く形は、動詞ごとのパターンで決まります。「The supervisor reminded the interns _____ their ID badges at all times.」では to wear の形が文型に合います。to 不定詞、原形、現在分詞、過去分詞のどれが必要かを、意味だけでなく構造から判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The supervisor reminded the interns _____ their ID badges at all times.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The maintenance request was handled _____ after it was submitted.
questions.explanationEn: The blank modifies the verb phrase 'was handled,' so an adverb is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 빈칸은 동사구 'was handled'를 꾸미므로 부사 'quickly'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 수동태 동사도 부사가 꾸밀 수 있습니다. 형용사 quick과 부사 quickly를 구분하세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The maintenance request was handled quickly.
Employees can submit a request online.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The maintenance request was handled quickly.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. quickly です。空所が文の中で何を修飾しているかを確認します。「The maintenance request was handled _____ after it was submitted.」では quickly が周りの語と品詞の役割に合います。動詞を説明する位置なら副詞、名詞を説明する位置なら形容詞、主語や目的語になる位置なら名詞を疑います。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然さより、空所の前後を見ます。「The maintenance request was handled _____ after it was submitted.」では、空所が動詞・名詞・形容詞のどれを説明しているかで品詞を決めます。

questions.prompt: Unless the client requests changes, the brochure _____ to the printer tomorrow.
questions.explanationEn: The brochure is the object being sent tomorrow, so a future passive form is needed.
questions.explanationKo: 브로셔는 내일 보내지는 대상이므로 미래 수동형 'will be sent'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: unless절은 조건을 말하고, 주절에서는 미래의 동작을 나타냅니다. 대상이 보내지는 것이면 수동태를 씁니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The client may request changes.
The brochure will be sent to the printer.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The client may request changes.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. will be sent です。「Unless the client requests changes, the brochure _____ to the printer tomorrow.」で問われている文法機能を確認し、英語の語順・品詞・決まった表現に合う選択肢を選びます。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「Unless the client requests changes, the brochure _____ to the printer tomorrow.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The airline will _____ passengers for meals if the delay lasts more than four hours.
questions.explanationEn: 'Reimburse' means to pay someone back for expenses, which fits the context of meal costs.
questions.explanationKo: 'reimburse'는 비용을 환급하다라는 뜻으로 식사 비용 문맥에 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 비슷한 re- 단어가 나오면 뜻을 정확히 구분해야 합니다. 비용을 돌려주는 것은 reimburse입니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The airline will reimburse passengers for meals.
The delay lasted more than four hours.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The airline will reimburse passengers for meals.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. reimburse です。語彙問題では意味だけでなく、周りの名詞やビジネス場面との相性を見ます。「The airline will _____ passengers for meals if the delay lasts more than four hours.」では reimburse が文脈と自然に合います。ほかの選択肢は似た形でも、目的語や場面との組み合わせが弱くなります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The airline will _____ passengers for meals if the delay lasts more than four hours.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: The marketing director congratulated the team _____ completing the campaign early.
questions.explanationEn: The expression is 'congratulate someone for/on doing something.' Among the options, 'for' fits.
questions.explanationKo: '~한 것에 대해 축하하다'는 'congratulate someone for/on doing something'으로 표현합니다. 선택지 중 'for'가 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 동사와 함께 쓰이는 전치사는 묶어서 익히세요. congratulate someone for doing something이 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The director congratulated the team.
The team completed the campaign early.
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: キャンペーンを完了する

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The director congratulated the team.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. for です。前置詞は日本語の助詞に直訳せず、後ろの語との組み合わせで判断します。この文では「The marketing director congratulated the team _____ completing the campaign early.」が手がかりです。期限なら by、正確な時刻なら at、曜日や日付なら on、範囲や場所なら in のように、英語側の決まった使い方で選びます。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 前置詞は後ろの語とセットで覚えます。「The marketing director congratulated the team _____ completing the campaign early.」の空所後の表現が、期限・時刻・場所のどれかを先に分類しましょう。

questions.prompt: The new packaging is more durable and _____ than the old design.
questions.explanationEn: The blank is parallel with the adjective 'durable,' so another adjective is required.
questions.explanationKo: 빈칸은 형용사 'durable'과 병렬 구조이므로 또 다른 형용사 'attractive'가 필요합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: and로 연결된 병렬 구조에서는 앞뒤 품사가 같아야 합니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The packaging is durable.
The new design is attractive.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The packaging is durable.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B. attractive です。空所が文の中で何を修飾しているかを確認します。「The new packaging is more durable and _____ than the old design.」では attractive が周りの語と品詞の役割に合います。動詞を説明する位置なら副詞、名詞を説明する位置なら形容詞、主語や目的語になる位置なら名詞を疑います。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然さより、空所の前後を見ます。「The new packaging is more durable and _____ than the old design.」では、空所が動詞・名詞・形容詞のどれを説明しているかで品詞を決めます。

questions.prompt: The team postponed the launch _____ several important tests had not been completed.
questions.explanationEn: 'Because' introduces the reason the launch was postponed.
questions.explanationKo: 중요한 테스트가 완료되지 않았다는 이유를 나타내므로 'because'가 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 뒤에 주어와 동사가 있는 완전한 절이 오고 이유를 나타내면 because가 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The team postponed the launch.
Several tests had not been completed.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The team postponed the launch.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. because です。接続語は前後の意味関係で選びます。この文では「The team postponed the launch _____ several important tests had not been completed.」の二つの内容が、理由・逆接・条件・追加のどれでつながるかを見ます。because はその関係を自然に示します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 接続語は訳語だけで選ばず、「The team postponed the launch _____ several important tests had not been completed.」の前半と後半が逆接・理由・条件・追加のどれかを先に決めましょう。

questions.prompt: The hotel offers _____ parking for guests staying more than two nights.
questions.explanationEn: 'Complimentary' means free of charge, which fits guest parking.
questions.explanationKo: 'complimentary'는 무료의라는 뜻으로 투숙객 주차 문맥에 알맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: complimentary는 TOEIC에서 호텔, 서비스 문맥에서 '무료의'라는 뜻으로 자주 나옵니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The hotel offers complimentary parking.
Guests staying more than two nights receive parking.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The hotel offers complimentary parking.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A. complimentary です。語彙問題では意味だけでなく、周りの名詞やビジネス場面との相性を見ます。「The hotel offers _____ parking for guests staying more than two nights.」では complimentary が文脈と自然に合います。ほかの選択肢は似た形でも、目的語や場面との組み合わせが弱くなります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 日本語の自然な言い方に引っ張られず、「The hotel offers _____ parking for guests staying more than two nights.」の英語の語順・品詞・よく使う組み合わせを根拠に選びましょう。

questions.prompt: Since the new policy was introduced, customer complaints _____.
questions.explanationEn: 'Since' connects a past starting point to the present, so the present perfect is correct.
questions.explanationKo: 'since'는 과거의 시작점부터 현재까지 이어지는 변화를 나타내므로 현재완료가 맞습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: since가 시간 접속사로 쓰이면 현재완료와 함께 쓰이는 경우가 많습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The company introduced a new policy.
Customer complaints have decreased.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The company introduced a new policy.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C. have decreased です。時制問題では、文中の時間表現を先に拾います。この文では「since」が動詞形を決める手がかりです。日本語では時制を文脈で補えますが、英語では過去・未来・現在完了などを動詞の形で明示する必要があります。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 時制問題では、選択肢を見る前に時間表現を丸で囲えるか確認します。「Since the new policy was introduced, customer complaints _____.」の中の過去・未来・継続の合図が得点源です。
